questions.question: Find the maximum distance travelled by a particle moving along a straight line in 8 hours, given that its speed increases by 2 m/s every hour. If the initial speed is 4 m/s, calculate this distance. (Assume constant acceleration.

A particle is projected upwards from the surface of the earth with an initial velocity of 20 m/s at an angle of 60° to the horizontal. Find the maximum height reached by the particle in 8 hours. (Take g = 10 m/s^2, and assume negligible air resistance.

An object moves along a straight line from one endpoint to another in 8 hours. If its speed varies sinusoidally with time according to the equation s(t) = 3sin(πt) + 2t, find the total distance travelled by the object. (Take t = 0 to 8 hours.
